summary: Iter 197 — Phase 2 Retirement Decision OS (cached engine + DecisionGraph + Compliance audit beacon + expanded rules R11-R17). Full backend + frontend validation. 8/8 backend pytest pass. All 9 acceptance items PASS on the frontend. The 'Too many errors' freeze complaint is RESOLVED — rapid navigation across 6 readiness routes produces 0 page errors, 0 console errors/warnings, no webpack dev overlay. RCC loads in 1.20s (under 1.5s target). DecisionGraph SVG renders with 18 <path> edges. Opportunity Engine surfaces 7 cards for thompson_family including SMSF (R12) and Life+TPD (R9). Compliance beacon fires correctly: 16 POSTs observed during the run and GET /readiness-events?client_id=thompson_family returns 8 persisted events. Cached readiness engine + 5-min TTL is working as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: compliance_audit.py /readiness-events POST validates client_id and returns event_id; GET supports client_id filter + limit; summary computes score_buckets/by_client/by_day correctly. In-memory store capped at 5000 with prune to 4000 on overflow — acceptable per PRD.

readinessCache.js 5-min TTL keyed by (clientId, hashInputs, hashOpts) and the audit beacon is debounced 30s per client — preventing the prior 'Too many errors' from per-render Monte Carlo. Verified: 16 POSTs across 6+ navigations is well-behaved.
DecisionGraph SVG renders 18 path edges from actions→factors→outcomes — visually present and structurally testable via [data-testid=decision-graph-svg] path. Good.
RetirementControlCenter cold-load via networkidle 1.20s — comfortably under 1.5s target. No backend dependency on render.
Frontend correctly uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL for the beacon (no hardcoded URLs). Beacon is fire-and-forget with keepalive — non-blocking.
MOCKED: /api/email-resend remains mocked (no RESEND_API_KEY) and compliance-audit storage is in-memory — both acknowledged in PRD.
Minor: KPI avg-score testid lookup returned n/a in one rapid-nav window because the script had already navigated away to /client-360 — not a product bug; iter196 confirmed kpi-avg-score=93 on RCC.
seed_data_creation: TEST_iter197_* readiness events POSTed to in-memory compliance audit (auto-pruned, no cleanup needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 batch (P0+P1) is fully shipped and verified. The cache layer (readinessCache.js) eliminated the 'Too many errors' freeze. DecisionGraph + expanded rules engine + compliance beacon are all working end-to-end. Backend route is /api/compliance-audit/readiness-events (POST/GET/summary). Two valid client IDs: thompson_family, chen_family.
rca_of_the_issue: Prior 'Too many errors' was caused by 200-300 sim Monte Carlo firing on every render across multiple readiness consumers. Mitigated by (a) computeReadinessCached 5-min TTL keyed on inputs hash, (b) reduced probe sim counts (200→120/100, bookAggregator 150), (c) audit beacon debounced 30s per client. Confirmed: 0 errors across 6 rapid navs.
